_metadata.description: Canvas API — Documented Surface. Strict source scope: Canvas-authored documentation only (canvas.instructure.com/doc/api/ + instructure/canvas-lms YARD docstrings). Mental models (3-resource rubric pattern, 2-step module item, classic quiz 2-IDs, NewQuiz LTI gap, course/account scope), universal patterns (pagination, parameter encoding, permission scopes, include[], HTTP verbs/codes), per-resource endpoint pointers. Paired with canvas_api_lessons_learned.md (empirical findings — separate file under strict source separation).
_metadata.runtime_strategy_rationale: Body ~15KB / 4 ≈ 3,750 tokens — within the 'either' band per runtime_strategy_rules. Set to read_at_runtime per named_override_patterns.selective_load: consuming agents load 2-4 of ~16 knowledge files per audit task. Selective-load defeats embed-cache benefits.
_metadata.version_state: v0.1 untested — per canvas-toolbox 0.x convention, not promoted to 1.0 until verified against a real Canvas course operation. Not yet wired into consuming agents' cross_references.knowledge_files[].

scope: Canvas's REST API surface as Canvas documents it. Mental models, universal patterns, per-resource endpoint pointers — all sourced from Instructure's own published documentation (rendered docs site + open-source canvas-lms YARD comments). Empirical findings live in the paired file canvas_api_lessons_learned.md. Out of scope: project conventions, canvas-toolbox tool patterns, GraphQL, LTI launch internals.

facts.term: D1 — Rubrics are three resources (Canvas data model)
facts.definition: Canvas's API docs model Rubric (template — criteria[], points_possible, free_form_criterion_comments, criterion_use_range), RubricAssociation (the join — use_for_grading, hide_score_total on the association, purpose grading|bookmark, bookmarked, association_type Assignment|Course|Account), and RubricAssessment (single scoring event — assessment_type grading|peer_review|provisional_grade, per-criterion points + comments, score, artifact_id, artifact_type) as three distinct REST resources. use_for_grading lives on the JOIN, not on the rubric template.

facts.term: D2 — Module Items reference content; they are not the content
facts.definition: Canvas's Modules API documents Module Items as navigation entries that reference content stored in other resources. A Module Item carries type (Page | Assignment | Quiz | Discussion | ExternalTool | ExternalUrl | SubHeader), content_id (the canvas_id of the referenced resource), and its own module_item_id. Creating the content and creating the module item that references it are two separate API calls per Canvas's docs.

facts.term: D3 — Classic Quizzes have two canonical IDs
facts.definition: GET /api/v1/courses/:course_id/quizzes/:id returns the quiz with quiz_id (quiz-engine view). GET /api/v1/courses/:course_id/assignments/:id with submission_types: ['online_quiz'] returns the same quiz with assignment_id (gradebook view). Canvas's docs document quiz_id is used for quiz-engine operations and assignment_id for gradebook operations including due dates. The two IDs are linked but distinct.

facts.term: D4 — New Quizzes are LTI-based, not REST
facts.definition: Per Canvas's documentation, New Quizzes is delivered as an LTI 1.3 tool, not as REST resources. Canvas Quizzes Next / New Quizzes documentation explicitly states content for New Quizzes is managed in the New Quizzes UI (LTI launch), not via the Quizzes REST API. Same for arbitrary ExternalTool items — the LTI launch surface is the integration point, not REST.

facts.term: D5 — Date field semantics differ by resource
facts.definition: Canvas's API docs document distinct date semantics across resources. Assignments and Classic Quizzes: due_at, lock_at, unlock_at (assignment override model). Discussions: todo_date (separate field on PUT /discussion_topics/:id). Conflating the two is incorrect per Canvas's data model.

facts.term: D6 — Course context vs. Account context
facts.definition: Many resources (Rubrics, Outcomes, OutcomeGroups, Files) can be created at either course scope (/api/v1/courses/:course_id/...) or account scope (/api/v1/accounts/:account_id/...). Canvas's docs note the permission model differs: course-scoped operations require course-level capabilities; account-scoped require account-admin capabilities.

Canvas's pagination documentation states all paginated responses return a Link header with relations current, next, prev, first, last. Consumers should follow rel='next' until absent. per_page query parameter controls page size (default low; max 100 per Canvas's docs, varies by endpoint).

facts.term: U2 — Parameter encoding: form-encoded or JSON
facts.definition: Canvas's docs state requests can be made with either application/x-www-form-urlencoded or application/json payloads. The docs use the form-encoded notation module[name]=value to express nested parameters. Both are documented as accepted. (Empirical caveats on this universal statement live in canvas_api_lessons_learned.md.)
citations: canvas.instructure.com/doc/api/ (general parameter encoding notes across endpoint docs)

facts.definition: Canvas's API authorization documentation describes a role-based permission model. Operations require named capabilities (e.g., manage_rubrics, manage_assignments, read_course_content). Student tokens carry read_course_content for enrolled courses; teacher tokens carry manage_* capabilities for course-level writes; admin tokens carry account-level capabilities. Example: GET /courses/:id/rubrics requires manage_rubrics — returns 403 for student tokens per Canvas's permission model.

facts.term: U4 — Include parameter for embedded resources
facts.definition: Canvas's docs document the include[] query parameter on many endpoints — request additional embedded resources in the response. Example: GET /api/v1/courses/:id/assignments?include[]=rubric returns each assignment with its associated rubric criteria inline. Documented include values vary per endpoint and are listed in each endpoint's docs.

Canvas's docs map HTTP verbs to operations consistently: GET — read (list or single); POST — create; PUT — update (full replacement on most resources); PATCH — partial update (supported on some resources); DELETE — delete. Per Canvas's docs, POST to a create endpoint with an existing-resource identifier in the path is implementation-defined per resource; some controllers redirect POST to PUT internally.

Canvas's API documentation maps HTTP codes consistently: 200 success; 201 created; 204 success with empty body (DELETE); 400 validation error (response body carries errors[]); 401 authentication failure; 403 authorization failure (token lacks capability); 404 not found; 422 semantic validation error; 5xx Canvas-side error.

Docs: /doc/api/rubrics.html, /doc/api/rubric_associations.html, /doc/api/rubric_assessments.html. API path roots: /courses/:id/rubrics, /courses/:id/rubric_associations, /courses/:id/rubric_associations/:rid/rubric_assessments. Survey: pre_knowledge/rubrics/canvas_rubrics_api_survey.md.
Docs: /doc/api/pages.html. API path root: /courses/:id/pages. Survey: pre_knowledge/canvas_api/pages_api_survey.md.
Docs: /doc/api/modules.html. API path roots: /courses/:id/modules, /courses/:id/modules/:mid/items. Survey: TBD.
Docs: /doc/api/assignments.html. API path root: /courses/:id/assignments. Survey: TBD.
Docs: /doc/api/quizzes.html, /doc/api/quiz_questions.html. API path roots: /courses/:id/quizzes, /courses/:id/quizzes/:qid/questions. Survey: TBD.
Docs: /doc/api/discussion_topics.html. API path root: /courses/:id/discussion_topics. Survey: TBD.
Docs: /doc/api/outcomes.html, /doc/api/outcome_groups.html. API path roots: /courses/:id/outcomes, /courses/:id/root_outcome_group, /courses/:id/outcome_groups. Survey: TBD.
Docs: /doc/api/files.html. API path root: /courses/:id/files (also /folders/:id/files). Survey: TBD (canvas_sync.py --pull-files exercises this surface).

Docs: /doc/api/blueprint_courses.html. API path roots: /courses/:bp/blueprint_templates/default, /courses/:bp/blueprint_templates/default/migrations, /courses/:section/blueprint_subscriptions. Survey: TBD.

Docs: /doc/api/courses.html. API path root: /courses/:id. include[] values: total_students, syllabus_body, term, course_progress, sections, storage_quota_used_mb, total_scores, current_grading_period_scores, account, subaccount, observed_users, course_image, banner_image, concluded.
Docs: /doc/api/submissions.html. API path root: /courses/:id/assignments/:aid/submissions. Includes rubric_assessment when requested.

sources: Canvas LMS REST API canonical documentation — https://canvas.instructure.com/doc/api/ (Instructure's published reference docs site).

instructure/canvas-lms — https://github.com/instructure/canvas-lms (open-source repo; YARD docstrings in app/controllers/*_controller.rb are Instructure's API documentation as code, canonical when the docs site is unreachable).

No empirical findings or canvas-toolbox tool references in facts[] or provenance (strict source-scope discipline)
